name: Grok-3 (Think)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (size undisclosed; trained on Colossus, ~10x Grok-2 compute)
note: Reasoning "Think"/Big Brain mode of Grok 3; hit #1 on Chatbot Arena at launch (~1400 Elo, per rdworldonline.com). Deprecated May 15, 2026, redirects to Grok 4.3.

results.notes: LMSYS Chatbot Arena Elo, #1 at launch [Corrected during full-corpus audit: xAI's own launch blog states verbatim: 'Grok 3 has leading performance across both academic benchmarks and real-world user preferences, achieving an Elo score of 1402 in the Chatbot Arena.' The existing corpus value of 1400 comes from a Musk quote relayed via rdworldonline.com ('achieve a score of 1,400'), which is a rounded/misquoted version of xAI's own precise, primary-source figure of 1402.]

Official 'Grok 3 Beta (Think)' bar in the launch blog's MMMU chart (Grok 3 Beta (Think) 78 vs Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking 75.4 vs o1 78.2). Note: corpus currently marks this model modalities:['text']/capabilities.vision:false, which conflicts with xAI's own claim of a Think-mode MMMU score -- worth a separate look at the capabilities flags, though that's outside this results-only report.

verificationNotes:
CORRECTED: the GPQA=84.6 row's original sourceUrl (futureagi.com) was fetched and does NOT contain '84.6' anywhere on the page (it shows GPQA Diamond 75.4%, which belongs to the non-reasoning/Standard mode). Multiple independent sources confirm xAI's official claim of 84.6% GPQA for Think mode (matching AIME 93.3%/LiveCodeBench 79.4%), and chatbase.co/blog/grok-3 was fetched directly and literally contains '84.6' attributed to GPQA alongside the matching 93.3/79.4 figures, so the sourceUrl was swapped to that page. AIME 93.3, LiveCodeBench 79.4, and MMLU-Pro 79.9 were each confirmed literally present on the original futureagi.com page via direct fetch. Arena Elo 1400 confirmed literally on rdworldonline.com ('achieve a score of 1,400').

[2026-07-16 gap-fill pass, core benchmarks mmlu/hle/math/humaneval/swe-bench]: Checked all three required source tiers -- (1) xAI's own Feb 17, 2025 Grok-3 launch blog (x.ai/news/grok-3, direct fetch 403s but its content is independently reproduced verbatim across multiple secondary write-ups checked: futureagi.com, valueaddvc.com, automatio.ai, medium.com/@sulbha.jindal) which consistently shows the Think/'Grok 3 Beta (Think)'/'Reasoning Beta' row carrying only AIME 2025, GPQA, LiveCodeBench, MMLU-Pro, Arena Elo, and MMMU -- xAI never published a formal Grok-3 model/system card (confirmed absent, unlike the Grok-4.x model cards that do exist at data.x.ai), so there is no primary-source MMLU, HLE, MATH-500, HumanEval, or SWE-bench figure for Think mode. (2) Independent leaderboards: Artificial Analysis's own 'Grok 3 Reasoning Beta' model page has zero recorded values for AIME/GPQA/HLE/MATH-500/MMLU-Pro/LiveCodeBench (all blank per a direct scrape of graysoft.dev's mirror of that AA page), and Grok 3 does not appear at all on AA's Humanity's Last Exam leaderboard; Vals.ai tracks only 'Grok 3 mini (Reasoning)' variants and a mode-ambiguous 'Grok 3' entry, with no full-size Grok-3-Think/Reasoning-Beta row on its SWE-bench Verified leaderboard. (3) Sibling cross-reference: grok-3-mini.json and grok-3-standard.json in this corpus have no MMLU/HumanEval/SWE-bench entries either; the one aggregator page with concrete numbers for full-size 'Grok 3' (designforonline.com/ai-models/xai-grok-3/, sourced from AA/HuggingFace: HLE 5.1%, MATH-500 87%, AIME 33/58%, GPQA 69.3%) has AIME/GPQA far below the Think-mode self-reported figures already in this file (93.3%/84.6%) and thus reads as the non-reasoning Standard config, not Think -- not usable here. Scattered aggregator claims of MMLU 92.7%/87.5%, HumanEval 86.5%/94.5%, and SWE-bench 42%/49%/63.8% for 'Grok 3' recur across low-quality SEO/aggregator sites (opencv.org, automatio.ai, serenitiesai.com, tokenmix.ai) with no consistent value, no citable primary source, and no mode attribution, so they were treated as unreliable rather than used. Conclusion: mmlu, hle, math, humaneval, and swe-bench were genuinely never published for Grok-3 (Think) by xAI or by the major independent trackers -- consistent with frontier labs increasingly skipping saturated/legacy suites for reasoning-mode configs -- so all five are left omitted rather than estimated.

[2026-07-18 targeted gap pass, hle + swe-bench]: Re-chased both current-gen gaps specifically, going beyond the 2026-07-16 pass's checks. (1) Official launch materials: x.ai/news/grok-3 still 403s on direct fetch; its content as reproduced verbatim across futureagi.com, chatbase.co, and multiple secondary write-ups contains no HLE or SWE-bench figures for the Think/Reasoning-Beta config -- confirmed again. (2) Independent trackers, fresh checks this round: Artificial Analysis's dedicated 'Grok 3 Reasoning Beta' model page (artificialanalysis.ai/models/grok-3-reasoning) explicitly shows 'Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index: 15 (estimate) -- independent evaluation forthcoming' with no per-benchmark HLE/SWE-bench numbers at all, and AA's own HLE leaderboard (artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/humanitys-last-exam) has zero Grok 3 variants of any kind listed. Epoch AI's model page (epoch.ai/models/grok-3) shows only an aggregate 'Epoch Capabilities Index: 139 (rank 84/211)' with no HLE or SWE-bench breakout, and no separate Epoch page exists for a Think/Reasoning-mode Grok-3 variant. Vals.ai has no grok_grok-3-reasoning model page (404) and its SWE-bench Verified leaderboard carries no full-size Grok-3 row. BenchLM's SWE-bench Verified leaderboard (benchlm.ai/benchmarks/sweVerified) lists exactly two Grok entries -- Grok 4.20 (76.7%) and Grok Code Fast 1 (70.8%) -- with no Grok 3 of any kind present. swebench.com's leaderboard content did not surface any Grok row either. (3) Academic/arXiv: targeted search for arXiv papers citing 'Grok-3' as an HLE or SWE-bench baseline returned no matching paper. (4) Sibling-model comparison tables: Grok 4 launch-era comparison writeups (datastudios.org, nitromediagroup.com, chatsmith.io) discuss Grok 3 only qualitatively ('Grok 3 Reasoning beat o3-mini on internal exams') with no cited numeric HLE or SWE-bench score for Grok 3 of any configuration. The only concrete numbers found anywhere for a model literally named 'Grok 3' -- HLE 5.1% and SWE-bench 42.0%/63.8% (designforonline.com/AA-sourced aggregator data, serenitiesai.com) -- carry AIME 33%/58% and GPQA 69.3%, both far below this file's Think-mode self-reported AIME 93.3%/GPQA 84.6%, confirming (as in the prior pass) that these numbers belong to the non-reasoning/base config, not Think, and remain unusable here. Conclusion unchanged and reinforced: hle and swe-bench were never published by xAI or captured by any independent tracker for Grok-3 (Think)/Reasoning-Beta specifically; both remain omitted rather than estimated.
